rows.summary: Rewrite acme-cart write paths in Go to collapse p95 add-to-cart latency from 180ms to 35ms.
rows.problem_statement: Cart abandonment is 71%, largely driven by perceived latency during add-to-cart and shipping-estimate. The Node.js implementation cannot deliver consistent sub-50ms p95 at peak (4x baseline on Black Friday).
assumptions: Go team can be staffed by Q3
Dual-write window is bounded to 8 weeks
Postgres pool size 50 is sufficient at peak

rows.summary: Reduce PCI-DSS audit scope from 12 services to 1 service via strict tokenization boundary enforcement.
rows.problem_statement: Current PCI scope spans the entire checkout pipeline, multiplying audit costs and slowing deployment.
success_metrics: Zero PCI scope expansion incidents in 12 months
assumptions: Stripe tokenization boundary holds
Envoy filter can be deployed cluster-wide
Egress rules do not break observability tooling

rows.summary: Roll out Argo Rollouts blue/green strategy to last 3 services (catalog, search, notifications).
rows.problem_statement: 3 services still use rolling updates which cannot deliver the sub-second cutover needed for peak weeks.
assumptions: Argo Rollouts cluster is healthy
Service teams adopt new workflow

rows.summary: Region-aware routing layer to ensure EU customer data never leaves eu-west-1.
rows.problem_statement: GDPR non-compliance risk and conversion penalty (18%) from consent banners.
success_metrics: 100% EU traffic lands in EU pods
assumptions: DNS layer can route by geolocation
EU pods can scale to peak

rows.summary: Instrument fulfillment, shipping, recommendations with OTel auto-instrumentation.
rows.problem_statement: Last 3 services are observability dark zones; root-causing cross-service incidents is slow.
assumptions: Auto-instrumentation libraries are mature
No PII in trace spans

rows.summary: October 2026 Black Friday rehearsal — drive 6x normal load through the full stack with synthetic traffic.
rows.problem_statement: Without a rehearsal, peak-week surprises will surface for the first time during Black Friday.
success_metrics: p99 latency within SLO at peak
assumptions: k6 test infra can drive 6x
Synthetic traffic is realistic enough

rows.summary: Stand up full active-active capacity in both regions with health-check-based DNS failover.
rows.problem_statement: Single-region failover introduces 5-15 minutes of degraded service during regional incidents.
assumptions: Database replication lag stays < 500ms
Cross-region bandwidth sufficient
risks: Cost of running two full stacks

rows.summary: Add Adyen as second payment processor with sub-second failover from Stripe.
rows.problem_statement: Stripe dependency is a single point of failure; outages during checkout cost >$100k/hour.
assumptions: Smart router can route by transaction type
Reconciliation tooling can handle both vendors

rows.summary: Add request coalescing and stale-while-revalidate to catalog service.
rows.problem_statement: 18% cache-miss spike pattern when hot SKUs expire causes DB overload.
assumptions: Redis supports per-key coalescing
Stale data tolerance is < 10 seconds

rows.summary: Define on-call schedule, escalation matrix, and comms protocol for the Nov 27 - Dec 1 peak window.
rows.problem_statement: Without a playbook, peak week incidents have ambiguous ownership.
assumptions: All teams commit to assigned rotations

rows.summary: Use a small fine-tuned LLM as the final rerank stage in acme-recommendations.
rows.problem_statement: Current collaborative-filtering rerank plateaus at 8% click-through uplift on personalization cohort.
assumptions: LLM rerank model is fine-tunable with available data
GPU capacity available

rows.summary: Two-week A/B test of personalized vs static homepage hero.
rows.problem_statement: We don't have data on whether personalization drives incremental revenue beyond the rec cohort.
assumptions: Treatment cohort is large enough for power
No novelty effect bias

rows.summary: Display loyalty-tier-aware pricing with subtle strike-through for discount tiers.
rows.problem_statement: Loyalty members churn because discount visibility is low.
assumptions: Tier data is accurate in real-time
Margin model supports strike-through display

rows.summary: Dynamic email body content based on last 30 days of browse + purchase behavior.
rows.problem_statement: Generic email content has open rates declining 2% per quarter.
assumptions: 30-day browse history is sufficient signal
Email volume within SES quotas
risks: Personalization feels creepy to some users

rows.summary: Tiered loyalty program (Silver/Gold/Platinum) with points accrual, redemption, and exclusive early-access features.
rows.problem_statement: Without a loyalty program, repeat purchase rate is below category benchmark.
success_metrics: 30% of repeat customers enrolled in 90 days
assumptions: Finance approves points liability model
Tier thresholds are achievable for median shopper

rows.summary: Migrate from sidecar-based Istio to ambient mesh (ztunnel + waypoint) to cut per-pod overhead.
rows.problem_statement: Sidecar proxies add 60ms p99 latency and 200MB memory per pod.
assumptions: Ambient mesh is GA in our Istio version
All sidecars can be removed without app changes

rows.summary: Extend active-active beyond storefront into catalog + checkout.
rows.problem_statement: Catalog and checkout are still single-region, blocking true peak resilience.
assumptions: Checkout state can be made region-agnostic
Catalog inventory is sharded appropriately

rows.summary: Central IDP on Backstage with golden paths for new services, deployment automation, and cost attribution.
rows.problem_statement: Engineers reinvent deployment scaffolding for each new service.
assumptions: Backstage is approved by security
Team has bandwidth to maintain

rows.summary: Deploy recommendation model's first stage to Cloudflare Workers for sub-50ms personalization at edge.
rows.problem_statement: Round-trip to origin adds 80-150ms before personalization decision.
assumptions: Model size fits in Workers bundle limits
Feature freshness can tolerate edge staleness

rows.summary: Enable third-party catalog enrichment via WASM plugins with sandboxed execution.
rows.problem_statement: Vendor enrichment requires custom deploys for each partner.
assumptions: WASM runtime is secure enough
Vendor cooperation on plugin authoring

rows.summary: Extend acme-checkout to support wallet-based payments via Apple Pay and Google Pay.
rows.problem_statement: 4-step checkout funnel loses 38% of mobile users at the card-entry step.
success_metrics: Card-entry step eliminated on supported devices
assumptions: Wallet SDK integration follows Apple/Google docs
Stripe supports wallet tokens

rows.summary: Tier-aware rate limits (Bronze 60rpm, Silver 120rpm, Gold 600rpm) at the Envoy gateway.
rows.problem_statement: Flat rate limits cause both abuse and noisy-neighbor pain.
assumptions: Loyalty tier lookup is fast enough
Rate limit storage scales with customer count

rows.summary: Publicly accessible order tracking with carrier-by-carrier status aggregation.
rows.problem_statement: Customer service tickets about order status account for 22% of weekly volume.
assumptions: Carrier APIs have public tracking endpoints
Order data is accessible without auth for owner only

rows.summary: Migrate acme-search to OpenSearch to avoid Elasticsearch licensing changes; identical API surface.
rows.problem_statement: Elasticsearch SSPL license change creates compliance risk for our distribution model.
assumptions: OpenSearch feature parity at our usage level
Index data is portable without loss

rows.summary: Standardize feature flag evaluation across all 12 services using OpenFeature SDK.
rows.problem_statement: Three different feature-flag SDKs in production cause inconsistent rollout behavior.
assumptions: OpenFeature SDK is stable
Provider swap is trivial

rows.summary: Add voice-to-search via on-device speech recognition (no cloud round-trip).
rows.problem_statement: Mobile search accessibility is limited for users with disabilities.
success_metrics: Voice search adoption > 5% of mobile search
assumptions: On-device speech recognition is sufficient accuracy
iOS/Android SDKs support the use case

rows.summary: Drop-in Redis replacement with 5x throughput per node; cuts $48k/yr Redis bill in half.
rows.problem_statement: Redis cluster costs scale linearly with traffic.
assumptions: DragonflyDB is Redis-protocol compatible for our usage
Migration is low-risk

rows.summary: Move from manual iOS/Android SDK regen (quarterly) to automated regen on every API spec change.
rows.problem_statement: Mobile SDK lag from API changes causes integration drift.
success_metrics: SDK regen on every API spec change
assumptions: CI can run spec-to-SDK generators
Partner consumers keep up with releases

rows.summary: Replace multiple REST round-trips from storefront with a single GraphQL BFF endpoint.
rows.problem_statement: Storefront issues 6-12 REST calls per page render, causing waterfall latency.
assumptions: GraphQL BFF can be operated within team capacity
Caching strategy is workable

rows.summary: Single search endpoint that fans out to catalog + inventory + reviews indices with smart merging.
rows.problem_statement: Search across product catalog, inventory, and reviews requires multiple searches.
assumptions: Index fan-out latency is acceptable
Result merging logic is maintainable

rows.summary: Visual query builder over audit events (filter by actor, action, target, time range) with export.
rows.problem_statement: Compliance team uses raw SQL to find audit events; turnaround is days.
assumptions: Audit event volume supports indexing
UI is workable in Forge design system

rows.summary: Playwright-driven smoke tests that run automatically after every production deployment.
rows.problem_statement: Post-deploy verification is manual and inconsistent.
success_metrics: Mean time to detect regression < 5 minutes
assumptions: Playwright scripts can be authored at the right fidelity
Test infra scales with deployment frequency

rows.summary: Enforce 200KB initial JS bundle; CI fails if exceeded. Forces tree-shaking discipline.
rows.problem_statement: Storefront JS bundle has crept from 145KB to 285KB over 18 months.
assumptions: Feature owners will accept bundle weight trade-offs

rows.summary: Migrate from generic Slack/PagerDuty bots to native Forge hooks surfaced in incident view.
rows.problem_statement: Generic PagerDuty/Slack integration is rigid; doesn't surface Forge context.
success_metrics: Forge context visible in incident view
assumptions: Forge hook runtime is mature enough
PagerDuty integration can be replaced

rows.summary: One-time ingestion of Confluence pages into the Org Knowledge graph (entities, links, decisions).
rows.problem_statement: Org knowledge is fragmented across Confluence; engineers can't find prior decisions.
assumptions: Confluence API supports bulk export
Entity extraction quality is acceptable

rows.summary: UI wizard for new tenants to walk through connector setup, RBAC, and starter seed selection.
rows.problem_statement: Tenant onboarding is a 3-day manual process with high abandonment.
assumptions: Wizard fits in Forge UI patterns

rows.summary: Visual diff between API contract versions with breaking-change highlights.
rows.problem_statement: API contract changes are hard to review; breaking changes sneak through.
assumptions: OpenAPI diff tooling is mature
Forge UI can render diffs well

rows.summary: Extract the 4 duplicate webhook handlers from acme-notifications, acme-search, acme-catalog, acme-fulfillment into a shared library.
rows.problem_statement: 4 nearly-identical webhook handlers across 4 services drift independently.
success_metrics: Time to add new webhook < 1 day
Single place to apply security patches
assumptions: Shared library packaging is acceptable to all teams

rows.summary: Break up the 31k LoC shared-utils package by domain (auth, observability, types) to reduce blast radius.
rows.problem_statement: Single shared-utils package causes cascading dep updates across all consumers.
assumptions: Domain boundaries are stable
Consumers can migrate incrementally

rows.summary: Pyroscope/Parca continuous profiling on payment, checkout, cart, catalog, frontend for flame graph analysis.
rows.problem_statement: Production performance regressions take hours to debug without profiling data.
assumptions: Profiling overhead < 2% CPU

rows.summary: Replace the .NET Framework 4.8 inventory API that handles less than 0.5% of traffic but blocks Python tooling.
rows.problem_statement: .NET Framework 4.8 is end-of-life; the legacy inventory API blocks our Python tooling modernization.
assumptions: Legacy API behavior can be reproduced
Python tooling modernization is approved

rows.summary: Provide Cursor licenses to every developer and standardize the team's AI-assisted coding workflow.
rows.problem_statement: Engineers use different AI tools; team workflow is fragmented.
assumptions: Cursor is approved by security and legal

rows.summary: Custom feature flag service instead of using LaunchDarkly to save $180k/year.
rows.problem_statement: LaunchDarkly contract renewal is $180k/year; build-vs-buy may favor build.
assumptions: Team has capacity to build + maintain
No advanced LD features are missed

rows.summary: Migrate observability vendor from Datadog to Grafana Cloud to reduce observability spend by ~40%.
rows.problem_statement: Datadog bill projected to reach $540k/year at our scale.
assumptions: Grafana Cloud can absorb our metric volume
OTel coverage sufficient

rows.summary: Cache tenant-prefixed LLM responses at the gateway for 5-minute TTL. Cuts per-tenant LLM spend by 30%.
rows.problem_statement: LLM spend is the fastest-growing line item at $24k/month.
assumptions: 5-min TTL is acceptable for our use cases
Tenant prefix is safe cache key

rows.summary: Sunset the /admin PHP storefront used by 3 merchandisers; migrate to the Next.js admin at /forge.
rows.problem_statement: PHP admin is end-of-life; only 3 merchandisers use it but it's a security liability.
success_metrics: Zero security findings from PHP admin
assumptions: Next.js admin has feature parity for merchandiser workflows

rows.summary: Replace REST+OpenAPI between acme-frontend and acme-api-gateway with tRPC for end-to-end type safety.
rows.problem_statement: Frontend ↔ gateway contracts drift; type mismatches are runtime errors.
success_metrics: Gateway API changes require frontend recompile
assumptions: Both services are TypeScript
T RPC operational story is acceptable

rows.summary: Build a dashboard in /admin showing security posture for each top-30 vendor (CVE count, MFA, SOC2 status).
rows.problem_statement: Vendor security posture is tracked in spreadsheets; refresh cadence is monthly at best.
assumptions: Vendor data sources support API integration

rows.summary: Replace 3 remaining SOAP integrations with FedEx, UPS, OnTrac REST APIs. Eliminates XML parsing overhead.
rows.problem_statement: SOAP XML parsing adds 50ms per carrier call and is hard to debug.
assumptions: Carriers expose stable REST APIs
Migration is reversible

rows.summary: Replace Node-based build tooling with Bun for 10x faster CI cache installation.
rows.problem_statement: CI cache install step is 4 minutes; block on every PR.
assumptions: Bun is production-ready for our tooling
Fallback path is trivial
